description: 30-case evaluation dataset for the commitment-keeper extraction skill. conversation is a list of {speaker, text} objects. expected_decision/expected_missing_fields encode the skill's documented rules, including the context-sensitive acknowledgement rule.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I'll send the quarterly report to Priya by Friday.

cases.notes: Self-made promise with concrete action, actor, beneficiary and deadline.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I will call the client tomorrow at 10am.

cases.notes: Explicit commitment with a named time.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I'm going to submit the expense report by end of day.

cases.notes: Concrete task with same-day deadline.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I'll fix the bug in the login module this week.

cases.notes: Accepted responsibility with a within-week deadline.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I will prepare the slides for the board meeting on Monday.

cases.notes: Named deliverable tied to a calendar event.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I'll review the pull request tonight.

cases.notes: Concrete review task with same-day deadline.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I'm responsible for onboarding the new hire by next week.

cases.notes: Explicit ownership with a deadline.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Can you review the report today?

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Okay.

cases.notes: Clear, concrete request addressed to the user + bare 'Okay' -> acceptance.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Priyanshi, please send the invoice tomorrow.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Sure.

cases.notes: Clear assignment + 'Sure' -> acceptance.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Someone should review this.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Okay.

cases.notes: Preceding message is not a clear request aimed at the user -> not acceptance.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: We may need to revisit this later.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Got it.

cases.notes: Ambiguous plan, not an assignment to the user -> not acceptance.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Can you review this?

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Okay, if I get time.

cases.notes: 'Okay, if I get time' adds a condition/hesitation -> not acceptance.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Priyanshi, please prepare the project report by Monday.

cases.notes: Direct assignment; user has not accepted.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Can you deploy the new version by Friday?

cases.notes: Request with deadline; no acceptance in transcript.

conversation.speaker: Manager
conversation.text: Could you handle the customer escalation?

cases.notes: Request without an explicit deadline; unaccepted.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Priyanshi, please update the documentation before the release.

cases.notes: Assignment with relative deadline tied to an event, not accepted.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Can you join the on-call rotation next month?

cases.notes: Request for future obligation; user silent.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Let's grab coffee sometime.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Sounds good.

cases.notes: Social plan, no operational obligation.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: We should go for lunch next week.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Definitely.

cases.notes: Social plan despite enthusiasm.

conversation.speaker: Rahul
conversation.text: Maybe we should look into a redesign.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Sure.

cases.notes: Vague suggestion, not a concrete commitment.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I wish I could help with that.

cases.notes: Casual wish, no accepted responsibility.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: If we had the budget, I'd hire another engineer.

cases.notes: Conditional/hypothetical, not real.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: If the client approves it, I might prepare another version.

cases.notes: Hypothetical with hedging ('might').

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Were we to win the contract, I would lead the project.

cases.notes: Counterfactual, no commitment.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I'm responsible for writing the onboarding doc.

cases.notes: Accepted responsibility, concrete task, no deadline.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I will take care of the server maintenance.

cases.notes: Concrete ownership, no time bound.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: I'll handle the refund for the customer.

cases.notes: Clear task, beneficiary implied, no deadline.

cases.existing_commitment: Send the signed agreement to Rahul.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Done, I have sent the signed agreement.

cases.notes: New message may evidence completion of an existing commitment.

cases.existing_commitment: Submit the tax filing to the accountant.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Just submitted it.

cases.notes: Short confirmation that may complete an open task.

conversation.speaker: Priyanshi
conversation.text: Renewed it just now.

cases.notes: Completion-language message against an existing commitment.
